name: Yugoslavia (1918-1941)
description: Three equal horizontal stripes — blue over white over red — covering the field.
history: Adopted on 1 December 1918 as the flag of the newly proclaimed Kingdom of Serbs, Croats and Slovenes, formed from the union of the Kingdom of Serbia, the Kingdom of Montenegro, and the South Slav lands of the former Austro-Hungarian Empire. The state was renamed the Kingdom of Yugoslavia in 1929. The blue-white-red tricolour combined the three colours common to the Serbian, Croatian, and Slovenian national flags, and reflected the wider Pan-Slavic palette. A state flag bearing the royal arms of the Karađorđević dynasty was used in parallel for official purposes. The plain civil tricolour was used until the Axis invasion and partition of Yugoslavia in April 1941; after the war, the SFR Yugoslavia adopted a closely related design with a red star added.

features.symbolism: the Pan-Slavic colours, drawn from the flag of the Russian Empire and adopted across Slavic nations during the 19th-century national awakenings
